title: Characteristic Polynomial Computation
description: Program to compute characteristic polynomial using Faddeev-Leverrier method
code:
def characteristic_polynomial(A):
 n = len(A)
 I = [[1 if i==j else 0 for j in range(n)] for i in range(n)]
 # Using companion matrix approach
 # p(λ) = λ^n + c_{n-1}λ^{n-1} + ... + c_0
 c = [-det(A)] # c_0 = (-1)^n * det(A)
 B = A.copy()
 for k in range(1, n):
 # B = A * B + c_{k-1} * I
 c_k = -trace(B) / k
 c.append(c_k)
 B = mat_mul(A, B) + scalar_mul(c_k, I)
 return c

def eigenvalues_from_characteristic(coeffs):
 # Use numpy for actual eigenvalue computation
 import numpy as np
 return np.roots(coeffs[::-1])
explanation: Computes characteristic polynomial using Faddeev-Leverrier method which iteratively builds coefficients using traces of powers of A

title: Cayley-Hamilton Verification
description: Algorithm to verify Cayley-Hamilton theorem for any square matrix
code:
FUNCTION VerifyCayleyHamilton(A):
 n = SIZE(A)
 p = CharacteristicPolynomial(A)
 # p(λ) = λ^n + c_{n-1}λ^{n-1} + ... + c_0
 result = ZeroMatrix(n)
 term = IdentityMatrix(n)
 # Add c_0 * I
 result = result + p[0] * term
 power = IdentityMatrix(n)
 FOR i FROM 1 TO n:
 power = power * A
 result = result + p[i] * power
 RETURN result # Should be zero matrix
explanation: Implements p(A) by computing powers of A and combining with characteristic polynomial coefficients, verifying Cayley-Hamilton theorem

title: Minimal Polynomial via Jordan Form
description: Compute minimal polynomial from Jordan canonical form
code:
def minimal_polynomial(A):
 import numpy as np
 # Get Jordan decomposition
 P, J = np.linalg.eig(A) # Note: only works for diagonalizable case
 # For general case, use Jordan form
 # Minimal polynomial: product of (λ - λ_i)^{m_i}
 # where m_i is size of largest Jordan block for λ_i
 unique_eigenvalues = set(J.diagonal())
 min_poly = []
 for eig in unique_eigenvalues:
 # Find max Jordan block size for this eigenvalue
 max_block_size = 1
 # (Implementation would require proper Jordan decomposition)
 min_poly.append((eig, max_block_size))
 return min_poly

def verify_divides(A):
 char_poly = characteristic_polynomial(A)
 min_poly = minimal_polynomial(A)
 # Verify min_poly divides char_poly
 return divides(min_poly, char_poly)
explanation: Shows concept of extracting minimal polynomial from Jordan form - each distinct eigenvalue contributes a factor to minimal polynomial with exponent equal to largest Jordan block size

title: Block Matrix Determinant (Sylvester)
description: Compute determinant of 2×2 block matrix using Schur complement
code:
FUNCTION BlockDet(A, B, C, D):
 # Computes det([A B; C D]) where A is k×k, D is m×m
 IF IsInvertible(A):
 # Use Schur complement of A
 S = D - C * A^(-1) * B # Schur complement
 det_val = Det(A) * Det(S)
 RETURN det_val
 ELSE IF IsInvertible(D):
 # Use Schur complement of D
 S = A - B * D^(-1) * C
 det_val = Det(D) * Det(S)
 RETURN det_val
 ELSE:
 # General formula: permute blocks or compute directly
 RETURN GeneralBlockDet(A, B, C, D)

FUNCTION IsInvertible(M):
 RETURN Det(M) != 0
explanation: Implements Sylvester's determinant theorem using Schur complement to compute block matrix determinant efficiently without expanding entire determinant

complexity.time: O(k³ + m³) using Schur, better than O((k+m)³) direct
